strike.note: only 7 held-out strike segments — insufficient data for reliable evaluation

architecture.conditioning: genuine sequence-level (exogenous target concatenated to every conv input)
architecture.model: TemporalGrabConditioner (1D temporal CNN + per-layer concat conditioning)

sources: CMU Graphics Lab Motion Capture (public domain, 59 clips)
Kyokushin Karate Multimodal Dataset (CC0, 1411 clips, 37 athletes)

head: 337fc3aa9a06ae614891e8d333169b42dc4ba4c8
honest_caveats: Strike FAILS: only 7 held-out segments, insufficient data (need 100+ per action)
Kick PASSES: median 6.07mm <= 15mm gate, 51.9% pass rate, kyokushin 80%
Grab PASSES: canonical karate 17/17 (100%), overall median 22.6mm
CMU grabs fail at 9.7% — need more grab diversity for full coverage
All three actions use the same architecture; differences are data quantity/diversity
machine_eligible_only: MACHINE_ELIGIBLE_FOR_LATER_HUMAN_REVIEW — does NOT promote, ship, or visually approve. G4/G5 PENDING_HUMAN.
objective: Train conditioners for the full motion vocabulary: grab, strike, kick. Each action family gets its own temporal CNN conditioner with concat conditioning + gate loss.
